# Contributing to Godimas101 projects

Thanks for the interest — every tool, script, and mod here is built by [Godimas101 (Chris Carpenter)](https://github.com/Godimas101), and community input genuinely helps.

## The easiest ways to contribute

**Report a bug** or **request a feature** on the specific project's repo. Every repo that doesn't have its own issue templates will inherit the ones from this repo (`.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`). Just click **Issues → New issue**.

**Support on Patreon** — [patreon.com/Godimas101](https://patreon.com/Godimas101). Not required, always appreciated. Chris uses Patreon like a running project log rather than a tip jar.

## Which org is this for?

Chris's work is split across a few homes:

| Where | For |
|---|---|
| **[Godimas101/*](https://github.com/Godimas101)** (personal) | Standalone tools, utilities, personal-projects docs, Claude skills |
| **[gitpush-mod/*](https://github.com/gitpush-mod)** (org) | All published game mods (Space Engineers + MechWarrior 5) |
| **[The-Canadian-Space/*](https://github.com/The-Canadian-Space)** (org) | Aerospace news project — n8n workflows, Python tools, wiki |

If you're not sure which repo your issue belongs to, open it on the one closest — Chris will re-route if needed.

## Submitting code changes (pull requests)

PRs are welcome, especially for:

- Bug fixes with a clear reproduction case
- Small, focused improvements
- Documentation improvements

Before opening a PR:

1. Open an issue first so we can align on the change (unless it's a trivial typo or crash fix)
2. Keep changes scoped — one PR per fix/feature
3. Test locally before submitting

## Licensing

Most repos are MIT-licensed with an attribution clause. Contributions are made under the same license as each repo. Check the repo's `LICENSE` file for specifics.

## Code of Conduct

Be decent. See [`CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) if you need it spelled out.
